Heather Altman.review_text: I feel like accounting can't be taught properly in lectures, definitely should be average class sizes. You can tell she does try when teaching. Exams have answer choices that appear when you do the wrong or not enough of the equations which messed me up as this was not the case in accounting 200. I would study for hours and it did not help on exams

The tests make up most of your grade. As a professor, she's fine. The lectures were incredibly boring and honestly not very helpful, which is frustrating when attendance is randomly taken. You'll learn more from the homework than from anything you'll do in class, so I'd recommend just taking the L on the attendance points and save your time.
She talks too fast and does not really care if the material is understood. Seems like she is there because she has to be.
So chill. easy content and lots of EC

Heather has been my favorite professor so far. She truly cares about her students and makes ACCT 210 much more manageable. The class is tough, so I recommend studying ahead and practicing regularly. Lectures are posted online, but even though only five attendance days are required, I suggest going as often as possible.
Professor Altman is one of the best professors for Accounting, just took it this Fall 2024 semester, she indeed is the best!
Very straightforward class and professor, homework was not hard and exams are fairly easy. Just show up to lecture and you will do fine.

Heather is a very sweet and caring person, she just wasn't my favorite professor. She clearly cares and puts a lot of effort into her lectures, but it doesn't translate well. She goes very fast and doesn't teach what's mentioned in slides or textbook. Lectures confused me so I ended up relying on myself to teach the concepts to do well on tests.

Altman was an amazing professor. Everyone knows ACCT 210 is hard, so anyone who wrote a bad review likely got a bad grade in the class and blamed it on Altman. If you attend class, do your homework, and study hard, you will do well in the class. Also, give yourself around 4 solid nights to study and watch the review videos!!! it helped me a lot!

I took Heather for 210 and she is one of the all around best professors I have had. Not only does she explain the concepts exceptionally well, but she is very sweet and it is clear that she cares about her students.

Overall, Heather gives very clear explanations of the material in lecture. The study guides, homework, and **lab assignments (highly recommend!) prepare you well for the tests. However, lectures are a little rough due to their length and her expectation for participation. It just gets awkward. Also, she takes clicker attendance very often.
Heather was one of the best professors I have had. She really cares about her students and makes the material very easy to understand. The class is difficult but she makes it so much easier

Altman is the best choice for 210! She makes you work for your grade as she goes through slides and material quick. You have to stay on top of the course and attend office hours/ lab to fully grasp material. Second test is by far the hardest but others are manageable. She cares about her students and give plenty of opportunities for help!

I did not enjoy having Heather teach Accounting 210. She zips through the class to cram as much material in as possible and doesn't leave much room for questions. Tests are difficult. There isn't too much homework and most of it is fairly easy. She is a kind person but I would not recommend her as a professor.

Heather Altman is one of my favorite professors at the U of A. She knows her material well and is great at explaining it to her students. This class is not an easy A, although an A is attainable if you are willing to work for it. Go to class to fill in slide notes, and there is an average amount of homework.

Highly recommend reading the book. She is a wonderful person, don't be scared to go into office hours. However, her lectures are boring and hard to keep your focus on. Try your best to pay attention in class because lectures do help and it's hard to teach yourself everything a couple days before the test.

Heather is one of the best professors I have had at the UofA. She is extremely clear in grading criteria and content. A very well-organized class. The material is a little dull but Heather makes concepts clear. Take her for ACCT210 if you are able!! Never used textbook, just get the wiley code and online textbook!

Unfortunately for the students, she does not seem to care about whether or not we actually learn and comprehend the course material. She seems to be one of those teachers that just clocks in and out of work/lectures and just goes through the motions.

Prof Altman is a very average professor. You won't become an accounting expert after this course, but you will know what's needed for the exams, which are in person, but not extremely difficult. Prepare to take notes, or cram if you're that kind of guy. Attendance is needed twice a week, and the location was rough, but it's manageable.

Heather did a great job explaining the concepts and applying real life scenarios to her lectures. She always had a positive attitude when teaching and provides tons of resources and wants you to succeed. As long as you pay attention, study and put the extra time in you will be good. She offers some extra credit at the end too. Take her class!

Altman puts effort in her lectures but I wouldn't consider her good. Due to the large amount of content she would skim over core conceptual to get to the calculations faster. She has great lecture videos, but the other teachers post the same ones on their D2L. Do yourself a favor and take the other ACCT 210 teachers (Katie or Alison)

Managerial accounting is hard. Given that, Heather does really good making sure we can power through it. She is a very kind person who deeply cares about hher class but since the material is extremely hard it may seem like shes a bad teacher. The lectures are long but the material is also long so it equates.

Unfortunately, the classroom is very very very crowded, which can make it hard to pay full attention during lectures. 
Additionally, I've noticed that the course relies heavily on tests to see how much we understand, and I find the tests to be quite difficult.

Professor Altman was a wonderful accounting teacher and is very knowledgeable in the content she covers. I found her classes to be very slow, which made it more difficult to focus, but she taught her content well and was great at answering questions. Tests will be a HUGE part of your grade, the extra practices are incredibly helpful for studying.

Professor Altman is a nice person, and she cares for her students to do well. I had an easier time in ACCT200 paying attention during lectures because of the teaching style however if you apply time to practice outside of class, you will do fine. Accounting is different for everyone though. Some people have to study for days and some not at all.

personally, she talked very quickly during the lecture and I got spoiled with an extremely small accounting 200 class, so it was hard to adjust. She is very kind during office hours and offers detailed answers that help you understand why you were wrong. Make sure you take detailed notes during the class and pay attention to specifics.

Heather is caring for the most part. The course format is almost identical to ACCT210. Lectures are monotone but she tries her best, and you cannot expect great excitement out of basic accounting. Study the slides and you'll be fine for tests. Overall, decent and easy course for what it is

Like someone else said, exams are like 70% of your grade and they are all taken with honorlock. Be prepared to do a lot of studying. Class average for most of our exams were 61%-70%. She offered 5 points EC if we took the student class surgery at the end of the course

Altman is honestly a great teacher for how hard managerial accounting is. You need to put in work outside of the class to do well & ACTUALLY pay attention in class, but she explains everything well. Exam & formula heavy, but if you study it's straightforward. Only complaint is she talks a little quiet, but other than that she's great & I recommend!

Professor Altman is amazing! She is very experienced and passionate about the material. She gives you everything you need to succeed in this class. Accounting is difficult but if you show up to lectures and study for exams you should be just fine.

If you like accounting and numbers, this class will be easy. However, the teacher is incredibly boring and spends 30 minutes at a time talking about things that could be taught in 5 minutes. Useless class as well.

I learned nothing in her accounting class, nothing. Exams make up 76% of your grade in this class, while homework, discussion posts, and other assignments make up 24% of your grade. You're doomed if you don't pass at least one exam well. She offered little assistance and had zero flexibility. In this class, honor lock is in effect, just a heads up

Accounting 210 is a difficult course to teach. But Professor Altman flew through the lectures to the point where it felt like she needed to get through so many slides so we could stay on course. My classmates and I ended up having to teach ourselves most of the material. If you out in a lot of extra work, you can succeed in this class.
she kinda just talks at you during her lectures, not to you. she barely explains and moves very fast when the class time is super long. the worst class I've ever had

You have to take acct210 and it's difficult. Everyone knows that. But she's the best professor you can pick. Explains things well, knows her shit, fast with email replies, respectful. You have to study each lesson and prepare for each exam. Attendance is random on D2L so NO need to buy those clickers. People who hate probably didn't study for exams

The worst professor I've had in all my years of schooling. That's not an exaggeration. She falsely accused me of HACKING/cheating on the zoom room for our final exam, failed me from ACCT210, and kicked me out of Eller. After I explained to her that I didn't cheat and was planning to get a lawyer to clear my name she took back what she said and did.

A hard class made even harder by mindlessly boring lectures, but I guess that's the nature of accounting. Heather isn't a bad teacher and knows the material, but nothing is really done to make it palatable. It's a put your head down and grind class. Be ready for the tests because they count for most of your grade. HW/quizzes aren't too bad.

Accounting is an incredibly difficult class. There is so much information and the tests are closed note making them very difficult. every other aspect is not bad -homework, quizzes, etc. but the tests are just awful.

She's great as a person but she seems like she's reading from a script. There's no change in tone. I get that Accounting is numbers abd concepts, but even when she's talking about real life examples, it's the same tone. Every sort of content is relayed like that so you don't know what content you're taking in. I had to rely on the textbook.

She will talk for an hour and a half and you won't understand anything because you'll fall asleep. In line with the school's accounting department, just plan to completely teach yourself everything. Unfortunately attendance is mandatory. Definitely try to take another teacher that doesn't take attendance. Class is definitely not easy.
She sucks. do not take. its like a difficult accounting class on steroids, gives lots of quizzes and work. Quiz averages are extremely low, and shes so boring. I give her a 0/10.

After transitioning to online she included quizzes which were hard and gave us very little time for them! Her exams always averaged to a D yet on the next ones she wouldn't make it easier or give us more time on it. Honestly, this class is a headache! I was 2% away from a B and she offered little extra credit.

She sucks, it seems as if the class got harder after the whole COVID-19 stuff. The quiz averages for the whole class was like 60% which is not even passing, and her lectures are the most boring thing on this earth. not engaging whatsoever, and her exams are very difficult. If u can skip this professor do so.

WORST TEACHER EVER! DONT TAKE HER!!!! I RECIEVED AN 89.5 IN THE CLASS AND SHE DIDN'T ROUND ME UP! COMPLETELY PATHETIC TEACHER, BORING, AND NOT ENGAGING AT ALL!!!!!!!! DO NOT TAKE! I REPEAT DO NOT TAKE

Pre- Covid-19: boring, wrote on paper notes in lecture, lacked explanations, voice is unbearable, hard tests, tedious hw. Now, mid-Covid: VERY difficult tests, ZERO empathy, HARD quizzes, not enough time to do lengthy problems, written completely different than how material was taught, cut NO slack despite the pandemic. she doesn't care about u!!!

Heather is really awesome and super nice. While her class is challenging, going into her office hours helps. She's willing to work with confusing topics with you and help you figure out ways to understand the content better. ACCT 210 is a little bit harder than 200 because of the content, but she tries new things to make the class easier.
lectures are painfully boring and it's difficult to retain any of the information with the way she teaches it.

In-class discussions are completely pointless. Hard to follow but you're required to attend for necessary attendance points. I never learned much from listening to her in class and would have to teach myself all of the chapters for the tests. When the online structure hit she made it much harder for students. After students were failing, she curved

When the class was in person her lectures were extremely difficult to pay attention to. It basically would consist of watching her take notes on a printed out powerpoint off of an Elmo projector on the front of the classroom. When everything got shifted online it basically has been the same thing but with an added level of difficult (added quizzes)
Not understanding whatsoever. Made class way harder after things went online for some reason. Horrible professor all-around

Professor Altman has one of the most inefficient teaching methods. I have not learned a single thing from this course. I even earned an A in ACCT 200 with a MUCH lower rated professor. I teach myself everything in this course. Not to mention, shes the only professor who made class/exams more difficult after COVID19 rather than lighten the load.

She's one of the best teacher in the accounting department, but it is a really difficult class. The class was much more doable before COVID, but the transition online wasn't the best and she did add more quizzes. She's great during office hours and very open w/ emailing you. She added a 6 pt curve to the exams to help. I would take her again.

Ms. Altman's lectures though very hard to sit through were not terrible but when switching to online due to COVID-19 she decided to change the entire course structure. This new structure making the tests even harder and even adding quizzes. It appears to me and many peers the class is no longer about learning and about her giving us a letter grade.

Heather is probably the worst accounting teacher at the UofA. In-class lectures are long, boring, and unenthusiastic. It's currently 2020, and she's still using a projector from 1945 to present a lack of material to the class. The tests are unbearably hard as she doesn't know how to actually teach the class. Overall an awful course.

I have been a student in her class in person and online because of COVID-19. In-person, lectures were extremely long and lacked energy. It was second to second of her talking. Now that all classes are online, she has decided to make the class even harder adding quizzes that you have no time for. It's very frustrating and would not recommend her.

I dislike her so much her way of teaching is so difficult she would always confuse me with her lectures. Literally one of the worst teachers I've ever had, I'm pretty good at accounting and I still found this class super difficult due to the way she taught. If you are thinking of taking her please SKIP. She sucks. I basically taught myself.

As a woman taking her course, she truly inspired me. Not many professors seem like real humans, but I love that she shares about her daughter. She also explains what she has done with accounting, she is very credible. She is so nice and not intimidating. She just wants respect and her kids to succeed! Best accounting teacher at u of a, hands down.
Very nice. Knowledgeable about the subject matter. Tries to break it down into easily understandable formulas. The formulas are all very similar & it can be very confusing.

She is such a great professor for this subject. She goes into detail and actually goes step by step to make sure you understand the information. I would HIGHLY recommend this professor. She makes in-depth study guides, all you have to do is put an effort into this class to make sure you learn the material and you'll do well!

Great teacher, recommend to do the accounting labs for extra credit. There is a lot of material to cover for this course so it will be lecture heavy so use the labs to synthesize each chapter. She is always in a good mood and will always give you a lot of in class problems.
Test heavy class. She is a good teacher, just goes very fast in class during notes.
Very hard class but heather is probably the best 210 prof at uofa. She is super nice and gets things done on time. You gotta work in this class.
I love heather, I never understood ACCT200 and in ACCT210 I am doing so amazing!! Definitely try and get her as your prof.

Heather is the best! Accounting is really hard, but Heather makes it easier to understand. She breaks questions down, does them in different ways, shows easier ways. Really a good, caring instructor. Go to office hours if you don't understand something, she will spend time helping you! Really cares about her students! 10/10 would recommend.

Altman is a great teacher! She goes over the material thoroughly and gives a lot of opportunities to do well in the course. If you ask her a question she will always answer it and lead you to the correct thinking!

This is another class that sucks but is mandatory. Super difficult and extremely boring, but Heather really cares about you as a person and it is obvious that she is a sweetheart. Aside from that, the class is absolutely dreadful and makes no sense. Super math heavy. Just pass it so you can get in to Eller!
ACCT210 is a more difficult course, but Prof. Altman is a great teacher. She cares about her students and is really helpful in office hours.

ACCT210 is a hard class but Heather tries to make lecture clear. Her handwriting is hard to read so make sure you listen when she speaks. Exams are tough but the guidelines are not clear as to what to study so read the book. She will not stray form the syllabus and it's "rules"

Good professor, seems kind and reasonable in class. There are clicker questions about once a week that you must attend class to get points for and random attendance points. The tests determine a big portion of your grade so make sure to study. She seems really knowledgeable and passionate about accounting which makes the class more enjoyable.

The teacher is fine but the class is HARD. You need to teach yourself and practice as you go and a lot. You have to practice, go into office hours, and just make sure you get it as you are going and don't wait till before the test. Attendance is usually whenever there are clicker questions, but there is random clicker question attendance as well.
Very easy class and Ms. Altman gives very clear explanations.

Exams are the only assignments that count for anything in this course.Exams make up over 70% of your grade. All other assignments count for almost nothing. During our very last exam she offered 1 point of extra credit if we did the course surveys, I'm assuming because most of the class was nearly failing.

Hands down worst professor I have ever had at the University. She is boring and does not know how to teach well. It sucks that she was the only ACCT 210 professor because I did not have an option to learn from a good professor.

Alison Mackie.review_text: The class was pretty easy, although you have to complete a lot of assignments. She was very caring and friendly, but the exams feel like you are the main charcter in Squid Game. There are at least 10 people watching every move you make at the exam, eventhoug there are 4 exam verions. Otherwise, the class was very good.

Pretty much whole class is exam based and exams are totally different then the reviews she provides. To add, she lacks any minimal amount of compassion a teacher should have, forced me to take exam 2 hours after finding out my grandpa had died. This tanked my grade and I was .1 away of a point from passing, she said it was out of her control. Awful

Honestly, do not take her class. She shows 0 compassion for students, she does not know what she is teaching, literally reads off slides, she does not care for you to succeed she just cares for filled seats and getting paid. I quit my major because of her, she gives 0 motivation, 0 understanding. No good words. Exams do NOT have cheat sheets!!!

Katie Maxwell.review_text: There is a lot of content you have to learn at a quick pace, so I highly recommend taking notes before classes and then following along more closely for additional notes during class. The assignments and exams are very manageable and pretty easy if you stay on top of the content. Professor Maxwell is also engaging in her lectures and nice 1-on-1.

Dr. Maxwell was clearly passionate about the subject and encouraged students to learn through practice, which I found was the most helpful part of class. She was always happy to take my questions and really cared about students learning the content. If you just listen and participate, you will learn everything you need to in class..

Sherri Silverberg.review_text: I don't understand why Sherri doesn't have good reviews because she is the best teacher that I've had. Choose her class! She always responded to my emails with a lot of thought and was always available to help. Managerial Accounting is hard itself and you will have to put a lot of time into studying but Sherri was always there to help and explain:)

Not sure where the sentiment in other reviews come from. Sherri is a great teacher who will bend over backwards for you if you demonstrate an earnest effort. This course is generally difficult, but Sherri goes to many lengths to ensure you know the content. She earns a 4 and not a 5 because she does breeze through slides like others have said.
very helpful teacher and wants her students to succeed. Office hours are very helpful. definitely a hard class but if you have questions ask or you will be lost.

Also, she graded the exams and then proceeded to add our final grade to our permanent record so that we, as a class, had no time to ask questions or even see our final exam. I have taken courses this semester where I learned useful topics and information, but NOT IN THIS COURSE. Do yourself a favor and do NOT take this class with her. -Ok Im done

Not one concept is taught using excel or any accounting software its just information and formulas that you will forget after this class. It should be setup from a conceptual perspective with more vocabulary and concepts, and less word problems that dont use most of the numbers in the question and try to TRICK students.

Exams make up 80% of your grade. If you have one or two bad days, your are toast. I received a 100% on clicker questions and homework, but still received a C in the class. There are so many things I would change, but it doesnt matter because this class is OBSOLETE anyways. No one does paper accounting, this isn't 1960s anymore folks.

First of all, let me begin by saying that I am an A student. I began this class with a 4.0 gpa. This semester has been exceptionally stressful and tremendously frustrating due to the actions and teaching style of Sherri Silverberg. I am not afraid to admit that I received a C, and I wanted to let everyone who sees this website to understand why...

Horrible teacher. She is confused half of the time she is teaching. You basically teach yourself in this class. You probably wont understand what she is saying until you get the practice problems, and you just reflect off the powerpoints. Save the trouble and choose someone else for Accounting 210.

I failed ACCT 210 so Sherri S. is my second ACCT 210 teacher and she is the best Accounting Professor out of the other two I had. The class is extremely difficult and you will have to put most of your time and effort in this course. Sherri is good at explaining and answering questions. She goes by the slides fast so take notes beforehand.

Shes uncaring and an uncommanding public speaker. Belittles students. I asked her to meet for homework help but she responded that she wanted to go home; shes so lazy! I also asked her to fix a mistake in D2L and she sent me a passive aggressive email about how she is on vacation - Bruh, its her job as a professor to fix grading mistakes.

Lazy professor & terrible public speaker! - She doesn't command the room or have a strong presence. Races through slides so students cannot take thorough notes. Exams are harder than study guides and more like in-class practice probs. She also made condescending remarks to me because she wanted to be lazy instead of do her job helping students!

Sherri is a nice lady but this class was not a great experience. It seems like she is always racing through the lectures because there are wayyy too many slides. She passes out practice problems but does not go over them. The study guides are just recycled versions of practice problems that do not offer any new insight whatsoever.

Sherri is an average professor, she teaches from the book exactly, which is rather boring and uninspiring. I started this semester with a 4.0 and thanks to this course my 4.0 is gone. She makes you memorize every formula for each test and she gives out practice problems for each chapter, but does not go over them with you in class.

One of the worst classes I've ever taken. She goes very quickly in class and does not know how to effectively teach material. You'll have to teach yourself because you won't learn a thing by going to class. Tests are a lot harder than in-class examples. Her study guides are vague and do not prepare you for what will be on the test.

While the material for the class is hard and yes sure the prof is kind of boring, she is still very nice. Even better she is accessible outside of class and that makes it so she can help you understand the material better.

I didn't understand a work in the class. She is confused all the time. papers are very tough. The slides are so hard to understand. It's one of the classes you have to attend but learn the content yourself. Dont take her she makes the class harder than it is.

She basically teaches what the textbook says (even examples are the same). She does not command the room, and is not very engaging. Uses acronyms and clickers way too much. And she makes several things due at once.

Most unhelpful, uncaring professor. She sent me multiple rude emails and brushed my concerns under the rug. I also tried make an appointment with her because I had class during her office hours, but she replied that she didn't want to meet! - She's so lazy. (Isn't it her job to help students?!)

An overall terrible professor. She raced through the slides, so people are lost. I put in long hours to study, but in contrast, Sherri only does the bare minimum. She rudely turned me down when I asked for HW help & sent me a nasty, disrespectful email. I also asked her to fix a D2L mistake, but she made an excuse b/c she's too lazy to do her job.

Sherri is really not a great lecturer, it is hard to stay engaged. It definitely helped to watch recorded lectures she posts on D2L, and go through the slides on your own. She does random attendance, though it isn't worth much of your grade. Otherwise the course is manageable and interesting, she does give extra credit.

Most rude person you will ever meet. Her lectures are so boring that you will wanna fall asleep and she stutters every 5 seconds you can barely understand her. Most teachers will help you out if your close to getting that next letter grade, she will absolutely not. She does not care. She should be more giving and actually recognize that she sucks!

Worst teacher ever. She acts like she cares about you but she doesnt. Because of her I am now changing my major. Dont think you will learn much, shes an old lady, but she teaches the course work so fast that you will need to do 2 hours of extra work to actually know whats going on. If you need an extra point to pass she WILL NOT give it to you!

Phillip Blanchard.review_text: Awesome professor. He explains the formulas, and concepts well. He has a vast vocabulary and will demonstrate it often. He will provide you with multiple ways to solve problems because he wants you to do well. Prepare prior to lecture by reading the assigned chapter and you will get much more out of the class. One of the best professors I've had.
ABSOLUTELY USELESS! PHIL NEEDS TO TAKE A COURSE ON HOW TO TEACH AND A SEMESTER LONG COURSE ON HOW TO TREAT STUDENTS LIKE ADULTS.

Very sad how he treat students. I got a high B in his class but couldn't stand his lectures. He is a lowly half-prof who is extremely arrogant. Could care less if you understand the material and takes great pains to try and confuse you.He seems to be mad at the world.

I was a straight A student before this class. I struggled with health related issues quite frequently during the semester and he did not ONCE try to help me (even though I'd attach doctor's notes). Terrible professor. Makes everything much more difficult than it has to be. You're better off watching Khan Academy videos instead.

Phil is one of the worst professors I have taken at the University. It is clear that he does not really care for his students and doesn't care to see us succeed. Instead he does the bare minimum and praises himself in the process.

If I could give him a zero I would, he isnt a bad guy, but it really irritates me that he is unwilling to work with his students. His solution to every student's problems outside of the classroom is to withdraw and he has no issues with being blunt. he had told us multiple times how we were bad students if we didnt score high enough. Just kinda sad

I understand why a lot of students don't like him. Blanchard was always nice to me despite me not having the best grades in the class. I think that's because I would try to show I cared about my grade and putting in the work. He provides tons of practice for every chapter. You can get the grade you want just study an hour from day 1 to the test.

Just take my word and do yourself a favor and take this class with literally any other professor. He's condescending and rude. He rambles more than he teaches and manages to throw in a snarky comment for every lecture slide he presents. As if his attitude weren't enough he is able to take simple concepts and make them confusing. Awful experience.

Hands down the worse professor I ever had. He is doing future Eller students a favor by retiring. He is very condescending towards his students and has no empathy for us either. When students would reach out for help we are shut down and doesn't try to even help us but rather tell us to drop the class. Literally wanted to off myself because of him.

Thank you, Blanchard for retiring. You are doing future students at University of Arizona a favor. You think that a Professor would be more understanding during a pandemic, wrong. He is condescending and rude to his students. Last I noticed Professors were the rejects of the world who couldnt find a good enough job. Get off your high horse

Blanchard is the definition of white privilege and teaching during a pandemic really showed that. He expected everyone to be so attentive to lectures when a) it didn't count towards your grade, and b) you can watch the recorded lectures. He would talk down on students who he thought weren't bowing down to accounting. IM GLAD HES RETIRING. BYEEEEEEE

Everyone hated him so much in the fall 2020 semester. He can be rude and condescending towards his students. If you show you care, he'll help you. He provides so much material outside of the boring lectures. For each chapter we are given practice problems and walk-through powerpoint videos. It's really a matter of you taking studying seriously.

This class is HARD and the live-online format only made it worse. You have to work for your grade and will not be successful if you can't keep up. Professor Blanchard offers additional lecture videos from another professor and these videos are very helpful. His lectures are long & boring. Just be ready to work hard and you can do well.

Please avoid this professor if possible. He is not very sympathetic whatsoever. He responded to an email from a student asking a question with a one word response. He also blames us for not knowing material or the technical difficulties that have been going on in zoom. Try taking this class with a different professor if you can.

THIS DUDE BLOWS. He is actually the worst professor i have ever had. He does not even try to be a good professor. Makes us watch video lectures that arent even by him and expects us to sit through an hour of him rambling about whatever. He doesn't answer questions or offer to help people. I've never been so confused. Totally unsympathetic and rude.

Terrible teacher with little sympathy, especially during a global pandemic. Ridiculous standards and measures taken for testing. Rude on top of everything. He's also very monotone and you will constantly fight off sleep in his class. I only took him because he was unfortunately the only teacher teaching ACCT210. Avoid him if possible.

Probably the worst professor I've ever had. His voice is incredibly monotone, and you will need to FOCUS on his dull voice for a brutal hour and fifteen minutes. The tests are difficult and you will think to yourself "did I ever learn this?!?" No, you didn't learn it, unless you taught it to yourself. Good luck with this man, Im praying for you.

Asks questions not on homework. Ineffective teaching style. His class felt like a prison because he enjoyed holding the students hostage. Do not be fooled by his easy-going appearance! He is very unreasonable and enjoys a tyrannical reign.
Blanchard is a poor excuse for a university professor. He is not helpful and tends to drift during his lectures. I am glad he doesnt teach any other classes I need. Avoid him if you can.

After taking many business classes at U of A, I would rate most of the professors as above average.However, Blanchard is the one prof. who I would rate as terrible.He is sloppy and lazy in his delivery and could care less if you understand the material. Maybe a little cocky, but a wonderful teacher

I can't understand why he won a teaching award. His students hate him, he doesn't relay the material well, and all he uses is Powerpoint.  Overall the worst class in the buisness department.  Go to Pima and get the credit and more importantly learn the material from someone who wants to teach
it is a hard class, however, if you listen to every word he says you will know EVERYTHIHNG thts on the test. i personally think he's great and i got an A. but i worked my butt off for it.

He is a jerk...plain and simple. Don't go to his office hours; they are a waste of your time. He is pathetic, loves himself, talks about how he was some psuedo war hero...the guy is a loser. Married his TA from a couple years back; over 20 yrs younger.  Talk about self esteem issues.

By far the worst teacher I have ever had.  He spends much of the class time talking about how wonderful he is and how easy his class is.  That must be why I have to retake the class.  What makes it worse is that he is the only one teaching the class.
he is a very good teacher. he knows what he is talking about and will be sure you know whats going on if you are willing to try. the class is difficult but he is very good

One ofthe worst professors I've ever had while at the U.  If you want to listen to someone talk about himself and how glorious he is day in and day out, take his course.  If you'd rather pass on his overstatement of his credentials, take it in the summer.
Testing format extremely difficult.  Good teacher but not willing to make exceptions to help students out.  Would suggest taking the course at Pima unless you are a stellar accounting student.
Has won awards for teaching style, but can make topics seem more complicated than they need to be.

Very straightforward teaching style. It helps to have someone straightforward if you are an over-thinker. Extremely thorough lectures. Just go to class it's worth it. Provides tons of additional practice.

His lectures may seem hard to understand sometimes, but if you actually look at the material presented it is clear. He practically gives you everything to do well on his exams. I failed this class the first time I took this because of personal reasons.So I was relieved after the first exam when I received a 90% because all I did were his supp probs

Very clever man. He knows what he's talking about. Listen to what he says about studying and try to apply some of his advice to your studying habits. For the tests he states "DO THE SUPPLEMENT PROBLEMS!". Not doing them will most likely result in a D or E but when you do them its easy to score an A or B. The MAL can be annoying but its free points.

Blanchard is really not as bad as people say he is. He wants his students to do well and does all he can to make sure everyone knows the material, basically gives the test questions in class. It is a hard class but just as long as you put in the effort, you'll do well. I don't know why everyone blames Blanchard for their poor grades in this class.

Easily the worst teacher I have ever had.  Likes to pretend he wants you to succeed but tries to trick you on homework and exams.  He talks down to students as well as other professors and TAs.  Blanchard is both a terrible teacher and despicable human being.

this effing guy. so acct 200, you can study and read the book as well, and learn and understand EVERYTHING. literally don't even have to go to acct 200 and just read the book. it is different for acct 210 because BLANCHARD is SO COCKY and changes things in the book, making HIS OWN terms, just very confusing. Very fast going over things in class. BS

Excellent instructor. Go to class every single day to get an A. There's a lot of flunkies that complain about him but I feel he's a straight shooter. That being said, I have very smart friends who got Cs because they don't like his "mean" teaching style. Again, don't cut this class.

Yes, Blanchard is hard, but not as hard as you think.  Just because he actually makes you work for a good grade does not mean he's a terrible teacher.  If you ever have a question, he's always willing to answer it, and he's a super quick responder by email. GO TO CLASS. He gives answers to tests, even though it might be a tad boring.

This is not an easy class and its totally necessary to go to lecture but the class is not really that hard if you take the time to understand it. Blanchard really does a lot to help his students understand and gives a ton of resources to help. You have to work for it, but an A really isn't that hard to get in this class.
Its really pretty easy stuff but Blanchard is HORRIBLE.  Make sure you read the book

Tests are difficult but he is an extremely nice man if you just go talk to him before class or after class. He will want to get to know you more and give you some great tips about the next test. Do everything he says and print out and study the material no matter what.

BLANCHARD IS AWESOME! THAT'S WHY HE WON TEACHER OF THE YEAR! HIS CLASS IS DIFFICULT, BUT NOTHING COMPARED TO THE UPPER DIVISION ACCOUNTING COURSES TO FOLLOW (FOR ACCT. MAJORS)! GET READY FOR MORE PAIN! ACCOUNTING MAJORS WORK TWICE AS HARD AS ANY OTHER BUSINESS MAJOR! I AM ONE SEMESTER FROM GRADUATING, AND ALL MY OTHER CLASSES WERE EASY!!!
good teacher

all_reviews.review_text: I feel like accounting can't be taught properly in lectures, definitely should be average class sizes. You can tell she does try when teaching. Exams have answer choices that appear when you do the wrong or not enough of the equations which messed me up as this was not the case in accounting 200. I would study for hours and it did not help on exams

The tests make up most of your grade. As a professor, she's fine. The lectures were incredibly boring and honestly not very helpful, which is frustrating when attendance is randomly taken. You'll learn more from the homework than from anything you'll do in class, so I'd recommend just taking the L on the attendance points and save your time.
She talks too fast and does not really care if the material is understood. Seems like she is there because she has to be.
So chill. easy content and lots of EC

Heather has been my favorite professor so far. She truly cares about her students and makes ACCT 210 much more manageable. The class is tough, so I recommend studying ahead and practicing regularly. Lectures are posted online, but even though only five attendance days are required, I suggest going as often as possible.
Professor Altman is one of the best professors for Accounting, just took it this Fall 2024 semester, she indeed is the best!
Very straightforward class and professor, homework was not hard and exams are fairly easy. Just show up to lecture and you will do fine.

Heather is a very sweet and caring person, she just wasn't my favorite professor. She clearly cares and puts a lot of effort into her lectures, but it doesn't translate well. She goes very fast and doesn't teach what's mentioned in slides or textbook. Lectures confused me so I ended up relying on myself to teach the concepts to do well on tests.

Altman was an amazing professor. Everyone knows ACCT 210 is hard, so anyone who wrote a bad review likely got a bad grade in the class and blamed it on Altman. If you attend class, do your homework, and study hard, you will do well in the class. Also, give yourself around 4 solid nights to study and watch the review videos!!! it helped me a lot!

I took Heather for 210 and she is one of the all around best professors I have had. Not only does she explain the concepts exceptionally well, but she is very sweet and it is clear that she cares about her students.

Overall, Heather gives very clear explanations of the material in lecture. The study guides, homework, and **lab assignments (highly recommend!) prepare you well for the tests. However, lectures are a little rough due to their length and her expectation for participation. It just gets awkward. Also, she takes clicker attendance very often.
Heather was one of the best professors I have had. She really cares about her students and makes the material very easy to understand. The class is difficult but she makes it so much easier

Altman is the best choice for 210! She makes you work for your grade as she goes through slides and material quick. You have to stay on top of the course and attend office hours/ lab to fully grasp material. Second test is by far the hardest but others are manageable. She cares about her students and give plenty of opportunities for help!

I did not enjoy having Heather teach Accounting 210. She zips through the class to cram as much material in as possible and doesn't leave much room for questions. Tests are difficult. There isn't too much homework and most of it is fairly easy. She is a kind person but I would not recommend her as a professor.

Heather Altman is one of my favorite professors at the U of A. She knows her material well and is great at explaining it to her students. This class is not an easy A, although an A is attainable if you are willing to work for it. Go to class to fill in slide notes, and there is an average amount of homework.

Highly recommend reading the book. She is a wonderful person, don't be scared to go into office hours. However, her lectures are boring and hard to keep your focus on. Try your best to pay attention in class because lectures do help and it's hard to teach yourself everything a couple days before the test.

Heather is one of the best professors I have had at the UofA. She is extremely clear in grading criteria and content. A very well-organized class. The material is a little dull but Heather makes concepts clear. Take her for ACCT210 if you are able!! Never used textbook, just get the wiley code and online textbook!

Unfortunately for the students, she does not seem to care about whether or not we actually learn and comprehend the course material. She seems to be one of those teachers that just clocks in and out of work/lectures and just goes through the motions.

Prof Altman is a very average professor. You won't become an accounting expert after this course, but you will know what's needed for the exams, which are in person, but not extremely difficult. Prepare to take notes, or cram if you're that kind of guy. Attendance is needed twice a week, and the location was rough, but it's manageable.

Heather did a great job explaining the concepts and applying real life scenarios to her lectures. She always had a positive attitude when teaching and provides tons of resources and wants you to succeed. As long as you pay attention, study and put the extra time in you will be good. She offers some extra credit at the end too. Take her class!

Altman puts effort in her lectures but I wouldn't consider her good. Due to the large amount of content she would skim over core conceptual to get to the calculations faster. She has great lecture videos, but the other teachers post the same ones on their D2L. Do yourself a favor and take the other ACCT 210 teachers (Katie or Alison)

Managerial accounting is hard. Given that, Heather does really good making sure we can power through it. She is a very kind person who deeply cares about hher class but since the material is extremely hard it may seem like shes a bad teacher. The lectures are long but the material is also long so it equates.

Unfortunately, the classroom is very very very crowded, which can make it hard to pay full attention during lectures. 
Additionally, I've noticed that the course relies heavily on tests to see how much we understand, and I find the tests to be quite difficult.

Professor Altman was a wonderful accounting teacher and is very knowledgeable in the content she covers. I found her classes to be very slow, which made it more difficult to focus, but she taught her content well and was great at answering questions. Tests will be a HUGE part of your grade, the extra practices are incredibly helpful for studying.

Professor Altman is a nice person, and she cares for her students to do well. I had an easier time in ACCT200 paying attention during lectures because of the teaching style however if you apply time to practice outside of class, you will do fine. Accounting is different for everyone though. Some people have to study for days and some not at all.

personally, she talked very quickly during the lecture and I got spoiled with an extremely small accounting 200 class, so it was hard to adjust. She is very kind during office hours and offers detailed answers that help you understand why you were wrong. Make sure you take detailed notes during the class and pay attention to specifics.

Heather is caring for the most part. The course format is almost identical to ACCT210. Lectures are monotone but she tries her best, and you cannot expect great excitement out of basic accounting. Study the slides and you'll be fine for tests. Overall, decent and easy course for what it is

Like someone else said, exams are like 70% of your grade and they are all taken with honorlock. Be prepared to do a lot of studying. Class average for most of our exams were 61%-70%. She offered 5 points EC if we took the student class surgery at the end of the course

Altman is honestly a great teacher for how hard managerial accounting is. You need to put in work outside of the class to do well & ACTUALLY pay attention in class, but she explains everything well. Exam & formula heavy, but if you study it's straightforward. Only complaint is she talks a little quiet, but other than that she's great & I recommend!

Professor Altman is amazing! She is very experienced and passionate about the material. She gives you everything you need to succeed in this class. Accounting is difficult but if you show up to lectures and study for exams you should be just fine.

If you like accounting and numbers, this class will be easy. However, the teacher is incredibly boring and spends 30 minutes at a time talking about things that could be taught in 5 minutes. Useless class as well.

I learned nothing in her accounting class, nothing. Exams make up 76% of your grade in this class, while homework, discussion posts, and other assignments make up 24% of your grade. You're doomed if you don't pass at least one exam well. She offered little assistance and had zero flexibility. In this class, honor lock is in effect, just a heads up

Accounting 210 is a difficult course to teach. But Professor Altman flew through the lectures to the point where it felt like she needed to get through so many slides so we could stay on course. My classmates and I ended up having to teach ourselves most of the material. If you out in a lot of extra work, you can succeed in this class.
she kinda just talks at you during her lectures, not to you. she barely explains and moves very fast when the class time is super long. the worst class I've ever had

You have to take acct210 and it's difficult. Everyone knows that. But she's the best professor you can pick. Explains things well, knows her shit, fast with email replies, respectful. You have to study each lesson and prepare for each exam. Attendance is random on D2L so NO need to buy those clickers. People who hate probably didn't study for exams

The worst professor I've had in all my years of schooling. That's not an exaggeration. After I explained to her that I didn't cheat and was planning to get a lawyer to clear my name she took back what she said and did.

A hard class made even harder by mindlessly boring lectures, but I guess that's the nature of accounting. Heather isn't a bad teacher and knows the material, but nothing is really done to make it palatable. It's a put your head down and grind class. Be ready for the tests because they count for most of your grade. HW/quizzes aren't too bad.

Accounting is an incredibly difficult class. There is so much information and the tests are closed note making them very difficult. every other aspect is not bad -homework, quizzes, etc. but the tests are just awful.

She's great as a person but she seems like she's reading from a script. There's no change in tone. I get that Accounting is numbers abd concepts, but even when she's talking about real life examples, it's the same tone. Every sort of content is relayed like that so you don't know what content you're taking in. I had to rely on the textbook.

She will talk for an hour and a half and you won't understand anything because you'll fall asleep. In line with the school's accounting department, just plan to completely teach yourself everything. Unfortunately attendance is mandatory. Definitely try to take another teacher that doesn't take attendance. Class is definitely not easy.
She sucks. do not take. its like a difficult accounting class on steroids, gives lots of quizzes and work. Quiz averages are extremely low, and shes so boring. I give her a 0/10.

After transitioning to online she included quizzes which were hard and gave us very little time for them! She sucks, it seems as if the class got harder after the whole COVID-19 stuff. If u can skip this professor do so.

WORST TEACHER EVER! DONT TAKE HER!!!! I RECIEVED AN 89.5 IN THE CLASS AND SHE DIDN'T ROUND ME UP! COMPLETELY PATHETIC TEACHER, BORING, AND NOT ENGAGING AT ALL!!!!!!!! DO NOT TAKE! I REPEAT DO NOT TAKE

Pre- Covid-19: boring, wrote on paper notes in lecture, lacked explanations, voice is unbearable, hard tests, tedious hw. Heather is really awesome and super nice. While her class is challenging, going into her office hours helps. ACCT 210 is a little bit harder than 200 because of the content, but she tries new things to make the class easier.
lectures are painfully boring and it's difficult to retain any of the information with the way she teaches it.

In-class discussions are completely pointless. Hard to follow but you're required to attend for necessary attendance points. I never learned much from listening to her in class and would have to teach myself all of the chapters for the tests. When the online structure hit she made it much harder for students. When everything got shifted online it basically has been the same thing but with an added level of difficult (added quizzes)
Not understanding whatsoever. Made class way harder after things went online for some reason. Horrible professor all-around

Professor Altman has one of the most inefficient teaching methods. I have not learned a single thing from this course. I even earned an A in ACCT 200 with a MUCH lower rated professor. I teach myself everything in this course. Not to mention, shes the only professor who made class/exams more difficult after COVID19 rather than lighten the load.

She's one of the best teacher in the accounting department, but it is a really difficult class. The class was much more doable before COVID, but the transition online wasn't the best and she did add more quizzes. She's great during office hours and very open w/ emailing you. She added a 6 pt curve to the exams to help. I would take her again.

Ms. Altman's lectures though very hard to sit through were not terrible but when switching to online due to COVID-19 she decided to change the entire course structure. Heather is probably the worst accounting teacher at the UofA. In-class lectures are long, boring, and unenthusiastic. I have been a student in her class in person and online because of COVID-19. In-person, lectures were extremely long and lacked energy. It was second to second of her talking. I dislike her so much her way of teaching is so difficult she would always confuse me with her lectures. Literally one of the worst teachers I've ever had, I'm pretty good at accounting and I still found this class super difficult due to the way she taught. If you are thinking of taking her please SKIP. She sucks. I basically taught myself.

As a woman taking her course, she truly inspired me. Not many professors seem like real humans, but I love that she shares about her daughter. She also explains what she has done with accounting, she is very credible. She is so nice and not intimidating. She just wants respect and her kids to succeed! Best accounting teacher at u of a, hands down.
Very nice. Knowledgeable about the subject matter. Tries to break it down into easily understandable formulas. The formulas are all very similar & it can be very confusing.

She is such a great professor for this subject. She goes into detail and actually goes step by step to make sure you understand the information. I would HIGHLY recommend this professor. She makes in-depth study guides, all you have to do is put an effort into this class to make sure you learn the material and you'll do well!

Great teacher, recommend to do the accounting labs for extra credit. There is a lot of material to cover for this course so it will be lecture heavy so use the labs to synthesize each chapter. She is always in a good mood and will always give you a lot of in class problems.
Test heavy class. She is a good teacher, just goes very fast in class during notes.
Very hard class but heather is probably the best 210 prof at uofa. She is super nice and gets things done on time. You gotta work in this class.
I love heather, I never understood ACCT200 and in ACCT210 I am doing so amazing!! Definitely try and get her as your prof.

Heather is the best! Accounting is really hard, but Heather makes it easier to understand. She breaks questions down, does them in different ways, shows easier ways. Really a good, caring instructor. Go to office hours if you don't understand something, she will spend time helping you! Really cares about her students! 10/10 would recommend.

Altman is a great teacher! She goes over the material thoroughly and gives a lot of opportunities to do well in the course. If you ask her a question she will always answer it and lead you to the correct thinking!

This is another class that sucks but is mandatory. Super difficult and extremely boring, but Heather really cares about you as a person and it is obvious that she is a sweetheart. Aside from that, the class is absolutely dreadful and makes no sense. Super math heavy. Just pass it so you can get in to Eller!
ACCT210 is a more difficult course, but Prof. Altman is a great teacher. She cares about her students and is really helpful in office hours.

ACCT210 is a hard class but Heather tries to make lecture clear. Her handwriting is hard to read so make sure you listen when she speaks. Exams are tough but the guidelines are not clear as to what to study so read the book. She will not stray form the syllabus and it's "rules"

Good professor, seems kind and reasonable in class. There are clicker questions about once a week that you must attend class to get points for and random attendance points. The tests determine a big portion of your grade so make sure to study. She seems really knowledgeable and passionate about accounting which makes the class more enjoyable.

The teacher is fine but the class is HARD. You need to teach yourself and practice as you go and a lot. You have to practice, go into office hours, and just make sure you get it as you are going and don't wait till before the test. Attendance is usually whenever there are clicker questions, but there is random clicker question attendance as well.
Very easy class and Ms. Altman gives very clear explanations.

Exams are the only assignments that count for anything in this course.Exams make up over 70% of your grade. All other assignments count for almost nothing. During our very last exam she offered 1 point of extra credit if we did the course surveys, I'm assuming because most of the class was nearly failing.

Hands down worst professor I have ever had at the University. She is boring and does not know how to teach well. It sucks that she was the only ACCT 210 professor because I did not have an option to learn from a good professor.

The class was pretty easy, although you have to complete a lot of assignments. She was very caring and friendly, but the exams feel like you are the main charcter in Squid Game. There are at least 10 people watching every move you make at the exam, eventhoug there are 4 exam verions. Otherwise, the class was very good.

Pretty much whole class is exam based and exams are totally different then the reviews she provides. To add, she lacks any minimal amount of compassion a teacher should have, forced me to take exam 2 hours after finding out my grandpa had died. This tanked my grade and I was .1 away of a point from passing, she said it was out of her control. Awful

Honestly, do not take her class. She shows 0 compassion for students, she does not know what she is teaching, literally reads off slides, she does not care for you to succeed she just cares for filled seats and getting paid. I quit my major because of her, she gives 0 motivation, 0 understanding. No good words. Exams do NOT have cheat sheets!!!

There is a lot of content you have to learn at a quick pace, so I highly recommend taking notes before classes and then following along more closely for additional notes during class. The assignments and exams are very manageable and pretty easy if you stay on top of the content. Professor Maxwell is also engaging in her lectures and nice 1-on-1.

Dr. Maxwell was clearly passionate about the subject and encouraged students to learn through practice, which I found was the most helpful part of class. She was always happy to take my questions and really cared about students learning the content. If you just listen and participate, you will learn everything you need to in class..

I don't understand why Sherri doesn't have good reviews because she is the best teacher that I've had. Choose her class! She always responded to my emails with a lot of thought and was always available to help. Managerial Accounting is hard itself and you will have to put a lot of time into studying but Sherri was always there to help and explain:)

Not sure where the sentiment in other reviews come from. Sherri is a great teacher who will bend over backwards for you if you demonstrate an earnest effort. This course is generally difficult, but Sherri goes to many lengths to ensure you know the content. She earns a 4 and not a 5 because she does breeze through slides like others have said.
very helpful teacher and wants her students to succeed. Office hours are very helpful. definitely a hard class but if you have questions ask or you will be lost.

Also, she graded the exams and then proceeded to add our final grade to our permanent record so that we, as a class, had no time to ask questions or even see our final exam. I have taken courses this semester where I learned useful topics and information, but NOT IN THIS COURSE. Do yourself a favor and do NOT take this class with her. -Ok Im done

Not one concept is taught using excel or any accounting software its just information and formulas that you will forget after this class. It should be setup from a conceptual perspective with more vocabulary and concepts, and less word problems that dont use most of the numbers in the question and try to TRICK students.

Exams make up 80% of your grade. If you have one or two bad days, your are toast. I received a 100% on clicker questions and homework, but still received a C in the class. There are so many things I would change, but it doesnt matter because this class is OBSOLETE anyways. No one does paper accounting, this isn't 1960s anymore folks.

First of all, let me begin by saying that I am an A student. I began this class with a 4.0 gpa. This semester has been exceptionally stressful and tremendously frustrating due to the actions and teaching style of Sherri Silverberg. I am not afraid to admit that I received a C, and I wanted to let everyone who sees this website to understand why...

Horrible teacher. She is confused half of the time she is teaching. You basically teach yourself in this class. You probably wont understand what she is saying until you get the practice problems, and you just reflect off the powerpoints. Save the trouble and choose someone else for Accounting 210.

I failed ACCT 210 so Sherri S. is my second ACCT 210 teacher and she is the best Accounting Professor out of the other two I had. The class is extremely difficult and you will have to put most of your time and effort in this course. Sherri is good at explaining and answering questions. She goes by the slides fast so take notes beforehand.

Shes uncaring and an uncommanding public speaker. Belittles students. I asked her to meet for homework help but she responded that she wanted to go home; shes so lazy! I also asked her to fix a mistake in D2L and she sent me a passive aggressive email about how she is on vacation - Bruh, its her job as a professor to fix grading mistakes.

Lazy professor & terrible public speaker! - She doesn't command the room or have a strong presence. Races through slides so students cannot take thorough notes. Exams are harder than study guides and more like in-class practice probs. She also made condescending remarks to me because she wanted to be lazy instead of do her job helping students!

Sherri is a nice lady but this class was not a great experience. It seems like she is always racing through the lectures because there are wayyy too many slides. She passes out practice problems but does not go over them. The study guides are just recycled versions of practice problems that do not offer any new insight whatsoever.

Sherri is an average professor, she teaches from the book exactly, which is rather boring and uninspiring. I started this semester with a 4.0 and thanks to this course my 4.0 is gone. She makes you memorize every formula for each test and she gives out practice problems for each chapter, but does not go over them with you in class.

One of the worst classes I've ever taken. She goes very quickly in class and does not know how to effectively teach material. You'll have to teach yourself because you won't learn a thing by going to class. Tests are a lot harder than in-class examples. Her study guides are vague and do not prepare you for what will be on the test.

While the material for the class is hard and yes sure the prof is kind of boring, she is still very nice. Even better she is accessible outside of class and that makes it so she can help you understand the material better.

I didn't understand a work in the class. She is confused all the time. papers are very tough. The slides are so hard to understand. It's one of the classes you have to attend but learn the content yourself. Dont take her she makes the class harder than it is.

She basically teaches what the textbook says (even examples are the same). She does not command the room, and is not very engaging. Uses acronyms and clickers way too much. And she makes several things due at once.

Most unhelpful, uncaring professor. She sent me multiple rude emails and brushed my concerns under the rug. I also tried make an appointment with her because I had class during her office hours, but she replied that she didn't want to meet! - She's so lazy. (Isn't it her job to help students?!)

An overall terrible professor. She raced through the slides, so people are lost. I put in long hours to study, but in contrast, Sherri only does the bare minimum. She rudely turned me down when I asked for HW help & sent me a nasty, disrespectful email. I also asked her to fix a D2L mistake, but she made an excuse b/c she's too lazy to do her job.

Sherri is really not a great lecturer, it is hard to stay engaged. It definitely helped to watch recorded lectures she posts on D2L, and go through the slides on your own. She does random attendance, though it isn't worth much of your grade. Otherwise the course is manageable and interesting, she does give extra credit.

Most rude person you will ever meet. Her lectures are so boring that you will wanna fall asleep and she stutters every 5 seconds you can barely understand her. Most teachers will help you out if your close to getting that next letter grade, she will absolutely not. She does not care. She should be more giving and actually recognize that she sucks!

Worst teacher ever. She acts like she cares about you but she doesnt. Because of her I am now changing my major. Dont think you will learn much, shes an old lady, but she teaches the course work so fast that you will need to do 2 hours of extra work to actually know whats going on. Awesome professor. He explains the formulas, and concepts well. He has a vast vocabulary and will demonstrate it often.
